title: What's New in Uniface 10.4.03

What's New in Uniface 10.4.03

What's New in Uniface 10.4.03

The Uniface 10 documentation is regularly updated to reflect new features, enhancements, bug fixes, and user feedback.

Uniface 10.4.03 consolidates new features, bug fixes and currency updates since 10.4.02.

Significant documentation changes done in 10.4.03 are listed here. For the complete list of new software features, bug fixes, and platform support updates, see Rocket Community: Downloads.

New Features

Support for non-Gregorian calendars

Uniface supports non-Gregorian calendars, such as Arabic, Persian, etc. from 10.4.03 onwards. To ensure that $date, $datim, and $clock works compatibly with versions prior to Uniface 10.4.03, you can also disable settings for non-Gregorian calendars. For more information, see $NLS_DISABLE_NON_GREGORIAN_CALENDAR.

C Callout now supports 64-bit numeric values

The C datatype ‘long long’ has been added to the signature editor for numeric parameters and return values in $status, in order to support the 64-bit C numeric datatypes ‘long long’ and ‘__int64’. For more information, see Define Operation Parameters in a Signature and C Implementation Data Types Properties.

A new utility cert.exe is available to help you prepare your archives for release

Deployed Uniface application archives can now be certified with public/private key pairs protecting you from unauthorized tampering with your resources. For more information, see Certifying Uniface Applications, How to Certify a Uniface Application, cert.exe, pathscrambler.exe, [ENCRYPTED_PATHS], and Uniface Messages 9000-9999.

Added two new Informix connector options

Two new Informix connector options 'table name size' and 'field name size' are added. These new options enable the use of longer table and column names, with lengths ranging from 18 bytes (default) to 128 bytes.

Note: The maximum value allowed for entity and field name in Uniface remains 32 bytes, where the entity name can be mapped to a longer table name in the assignment file.

For more information, see Informix Connector Options, field name size, table name size, and DBMS Connector Limits.

Deprecated statements in Uniface 10.4.03

The following Uniface statements are deprecated and superseded by their respective $<function>:

Deprecated statement Respective $<Function>
fieldvideo $fieldvideo
fieldsyntax $fieldsyntax
displaylength $displaylength
length $length
scan $scan
lowercase $lowercase
uppercase $uppercase

Uniface UX - Enhancements and Additions

Enhancements

  • The Uniface UX field widgets are now Generally Available; they are no longer in the experimental phase.
  • The Uniface UX widget's property inspector does not include any style related properties. To add some styling to a UX widget, use the respective CSS selectors of the widget as referred in the documentation. For more information, see respective UX physical widgets details Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages.
  • The UX widgets have an explicit list of supported HTML properties. For more information, see respective UX physical widgets details Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages.
  • The property names of ux-TextField and ux-NumberField widgets that refer to the change button have been changed. For more information, see uxtextfield and uxnumberfield.
  • The name of the format property of ux-PlainText is changed to plaintext-format. For more information, see uxplaintext.
  • You can now define several trigger mappings to a single trigger. For more information, see mapTrigger.
  • The Entity/Field object definitions are now passed with the processLayout() API function, allowing UX widgets to generate a layout based on object structure and properties. For more details, see processLayout and Example: UX Widget processLayout().

New additions

  • Uniface introduces the 'UX Entity Widget Interface' as an experimental feature. Using the UX Entity Widget Interface, developers can create their own UX Entity Widgets allowing a powerful mechanism to reuse custom build UX constructions. This feature is experimental. You can currently use it to explore the possibilities of creating your own Entity Widgets for your applications, learn about the potential and added value that this feature might bring to your projects. For more information, see UX Widget Interface or enroll into E-Learning course.
  • The ux-DataGrid widget is a new widget that has been added as experimental release. ux-DataGrid widget is the first entity-level widget to be added to the set of UX widgets. Although the current version of ux-DataGrid widget shows limited functionality, it demonstrates the end-less possibilities of entity widgets. For more details, see uxdatagrid and ux-DataGrid.
  • The ux-Radiogroup widget is also a new field-level widget that has been added to the set UX widgets. For more information, see uxradiogroup and ux-RadioGroup.
  • As a developer, you can now fire both server-side and client-side collection and occurrence (web)triggers for a UX entity widget, so that such client events can be handled on the server.

    A collection-level trigger can be fired from an uent-bound element using the collection widget class, whereas an occurrence level trigger can be fired from an uocc-bound element using the occurrence widget class. For more details, see Triggers in Dynamic Server Pages, Implementing External Client-Side Triggers and Operations, Data Format of External Client-Side Triggers and Operations, and UX Widget Interface.

Other Uniface Enhancements:

  • Bundling UX widgets

    In the experimental release of the UX web widgets in patch 10.4.02.037, all UX widgets related JS and CSS sources were released as individual files. For performance and maintainability, all individual UX widget files are now delivered as bundles. The FluentUI web components are combined with the UX widgets related JS files in the minified JS bundle unifaceux.min.js. The Fabric CSS is combined with the UX widgets related CSS files in the minified CSS bundle unifaceux.min.css. Both bundles are in the common ux folder.

    To improve performance and reliability (as no internet access is needed) the set of icons used by the UX widgets is provided in common/ux.

    Custom widgets can be created with different names and new bundles, so that they do not interfere with the bundled Uniface UX widgets.

  • Currency
    • Upgrade to Tomcat 10.1
      The version of Apache Tomcat delivered with the Uniface installation has been upgraded to Tomcat 10.1. The following changes may affect your web server configuration:
      • Java 11 or higher is now required. If you are running, for example on Java 8 (for Tomcat 9), you must upgrade Java to at least version 11. The OpenJDK provided with Uniface has already been upgraded to Java 17.
      • Supported Servlet specification is now 6.0, which requires that the web server be compliant with the HTTP/2 protocol. Most modern web browsers are already compliant with this protocol. The Uniface servlets Web Request Dispatcher (WRD v7) and SOAP Request Dispatcher (SRD) have also been updated to support the Servlet 6.0 specification, but the WRD v6 can still be used with web servers that support the Servlet 3.0 specification.
      • We upgraded from Servlet 3.0 to Servlet 6.0 to leverage enhanced features and improved performance. Servlet 6.0 offers better support for HTTP/2, asynchronous processing, and security enhancements. This upgrade ensures applications are more scalable and secure. Additionally, it aligns with modern web standards and practices. Overall, this transition helps us deliver a more robust and efficient user experience. This also includes package name update javax.servlet to jakarta.servlet.
      Note: Tomcat 10.1 is the default web server when installing Uniface 10.4.03. Upgrading to 10.4.03 will not replace any existing Tomcat configuration.
    • Removed IIS configuration from Uniface installer

      When installing the Uniface Enterprise Edition the 'Configure IIS for use with Uniface' option will no longer be included in the installation process. Although the IIS feature is no longer available in the Uniface installation process, it is still possible to manually configure IIS following the Uniface documentation.

    • OpenJDK 17

      OpenJDK 17 is installed as part of a Uniface 10.4.03 Enterprise Edition installation on Windows, replacing OpenJDK 8. OpenJDK 17 is the default for a new Uniface installation. When upgrading to 10.4.03, the installation will not replace existing JRE and Tomcat configuration.

      Uniface uses the Java Runtime Environment (JRE) for selected functionality, such as running Jakarta servlets for Uniface web applications and web services, parsing XML, and for Java call-in. During installation, you can choose to install the JRE provided with Uniface, or you can have Uniface use an existing JRE installation.

    • Java Call-In

      Java Call-In module has been updated with the following changes:

      • The package name is changed from com.compuware.uniface to com.rocketsoftware.uniface.jci
      • The name of the jar file is changed from ujci20.jar to ujci30.jar.

        The previous version, using ujci20.jar, is not supported.

        We advise you to update your Java modules accordingly. You either generate new wrapper classes or update your java wrapper classes manually and rebuild your module.

    • Web license feature checkout

      With the latest version of USYSHTTP, it is easier to activate a component that is not a DSP or a USP from a web client. Regardless of the component type, the USRVUSP feature is now checked out when activated from a web client, so not only DSP and USP but also in case of, for example, a REST service. As of 10.4.03, the license feature USRVUSP is checked out the moment the WRD activates USYSHTTP.

    • Compatibility of WRD.jar

      Prior to Uniface 10.4.03, the patching process copied the newest wrd.jar of WRD 6 into the installation directory overwriting the wrd.jar of the existing installation. This worked for Uniface 10.4.02 and earlier, which shipped Tomcat 9 based on Servlet 4. In 10.4.03, Uniface comes with Tomcat 10.1 based on Servlet 6 and a compliant wrd.jar (WRD 7.0 or later), which is not compatible with Tomcat 9.

      Copying an incompatible wrd.jar in the following cases would break existing installations:

      • when Uniface 10.4.03 with Tomcat 10.1 is already in place.
      • when a 10.4.02, or earlier, installation has manually been migrated to Tomcat 10.1.

        The reason of incompatibility is that WRD 6 uses namespace com.compuware.uniface which works on tomcat 9 (uniface 10.4.02 is shipped with tomcat 9) and WRD 7 uses com.rocketsoftware.uniface which works as namespace on tomcat 10.1 (uniface 10.4.03 is shipped with tomcat 10.1). For this reason, patching no longer includes copying a version of wrd.jar in the default location common/tomcat/lib (both wrd.jar files). Instead, a wrd.jar (version 6.4 or latest v6) is copied to uniface/webapps/uniface/WEB-INF/lib_dev/v6/wrd.jar and a wrd.jar (version 7.1 or latest v7) is copied in uniface/webapps/uniface/WEB-INF/lib_dev/v7/wrd.jar.

    • ICU is upgraded to version 74.2

      In 10.4.03, ICU is upgraded to 74.2. This version includes bug fixes and updates to CLDR 44.1. Note that, there are some changes due to this upgrade, such as:

      • The currency format for English (South Africa) has changed.
      • A no-break space is introduced in time formatting.
      • The month abbreviation in the date format for Dutch (Netherlands) has changed.
      • The year of 2 digits becomes 4 digits.
      • More locales and time zones are now supported, with the exception of the new ff_Adlm locale in CLDR.

      For more information, see ICU and CLDR documentation.

    • Platform Support

      The following platforms are no longer supported:

      • CentOS 7.* (64-bit)
      • Red Hat Linux Enterprise edition 7.* (64-bit)
      • SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 12.5 (64-bit)
      • SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15.2 – 15.4 (64-bit)
      • IBM RS/6000 / pSeries, AIX 7.1 (64-bit)
      • IBM i 7.3 on iSeries
      • Windows 8.1
      • Windows Server 2012

      The following databases are no longer supported:

      • DB2 v10
      • PostgreSQL 10
      • PostgreSQL 11
      • PostgreSQL 12
      • SQLite 3.35.5
      • SQLite 3.7.17

      The following database connectors are no longer supported:

      • DB2 U4.0
      • PGS U1.2
      • PGS U1.3
      • PGS U1.4

      The following browser is no longer supported:

      • Internet Explorer 11

      The following new platform is supported:

      • SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15.5* (64-bit)

      For more information, see Platform Availability Matrix.

Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
